spec_match.rationale: The page claims to style thumbnails automatically using :playing and :paused CSS pseudo-classes without JavaScript class toggling. However, the code loads empty <video> tags and relies entirely on JS click handlers to manually write inline styling overrides (e.g. thumb.style.background = '#dbeafe'), bypassing the platform feature entirely.

interactive.rationale: Features interactive play, pause, and mute control toggles with fake progress bar timers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Explains the desired CSS sibling patterns conceptually.

references.rationale: Missing the mandatory reference link to the chromestatus.com/feature/5068277495758848 entry.

design_system.rationale: Conforms cleanly to the standard page layouts.

openQuestions.title: Use real video elements styled purely by CSS
openQuestions.detail: Remove the inline style overrides (thumb.style.background and badge.style.cssText) inside JS. Supply a valid short looped silent MP4 data-uri or webm stream to each <video> element, call vid.play() / vid.pause() / vid.muted = true natively, and let the CSS :playing and :paused pseudo-classes style the sibling cards purely as described in the CSS annotations.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5068277495758848 at the bottom of the page.

summary: The video-gallery concept fails conformance because it mocks the media states via inline JS styles rather than demonstrating real CSS selectors on valid video streams, and is missing the ChromeStatus link.
